title: Brookfield Agrees €1.8bn Acquisition of German Offshore Wind Portfolio
summary: Brookfield Asset Management has agreed to acquire a 680MW offshore wind portfolio across the German North Sea from RWE, in one of the largest clean-energy transactions in Europe this year. The deal underscores continued PE appetite for stabilised renewable assets with long-term contracted revenues, and is expected to close in Q3 2026.

title: European Commission Opens €12bn InvestEU Infrastructure Window for 2026–2028
summary: The European Commission has launched a new €12bn InvestEU blended-finance window targeting transport decarbonisation, digital infrastructure, and social housing across EU member states. Co-investment structures are designed to crowd in private capital, creating significant deal-flow opportunities for infrastructure funds with EU market exposure.

title: KKR Acquires Majority Stake in French Fibre-to-the-Home Network Operator
summary: KKR Infrastructure has agreed to acquire a 60% stake in Axione Réseaux, a French FTTH operator covering 2.1 million homes across rural and peri-urban zones. The investment reflects growing PE conviction in regulated digital infrastructure with government subsidy backstops, a theme that has driven valuations sharply higher across Western Europe.

title: EIB Backs €900m Polish Rail Electrification Programme with Sovereign Guarantee
summary: The European Investment Bank has approved a €900m loan to PKP PLK to electrify 1,400km of Polish rail network, backed by a Polish sovereign counter-guarantee. The project opens refinancing and mezzanine co-investment opportunities for infrastructure debt funds with appetite for investment-grade Central European exposure.

title: Stockholm Data Centre Corridor Attracts €3bn Investment Commitments from US Hyperscalers
summary: Microsoft, Amazon, and Google have collectively committed €3bn to expand hyperscale data centre capacity in the Stockholm–Eskilstuna corridor, exploiting access to cheap hydroelectric power and a stable regulatory environment. The pipeline creates sub-supply-chain opportunities for Nordic infrastructure funds in power delivery, cooling, and connectivity.

title: Spain Launches PPP Tender for 280km Mediterranean Corridor High-Speed Rail Section
summary: Spain's ADIF has launched a PPP tender for a 280km high-speed rail section linking Valencia to Murcia, with an estimated capital value of €4.2bn and a 35-year concession period. The structure includes availability payments from the Spanish state, making it attractive to core infrastructure funds seeking long-dated, government-backed returns.

title: Macquarie and Meridiam Shortlisted for €650m Dutch Water Network Privatisation
summary: Macquarie Asset Management and Meridiam have been shortlisted for the acquisition of Vitens Noord, a Dutch regional water distribution network serving 1.1 million connections, as the Dutch government tests partial utility privatisation under new EU water security rules. A final decision is expected before year-end, with a transaction value estimated at €650m.

title: UK Government Sells £1.1bn Stake in Thames Tideway Tunnel to Pension Consortium
summary: The UK Infrastructure Bank has completed the sale of its £1.1bn equity position in the Thames Tideway Tunnel super-sewer project to a consortium of Canadian and Australian pension funds. The transaction sets a precedent for government recycling of capital out of infrastructure assets post-construction.

title: Iberdrola Closes €2.4bn Green Bond for Iberian Solar Pipeline
summary: Iberdrola has priced a €2.4bn green bond to finance its 4GW Iberian solar and storage development pipeline through 2028. The oversubscribed issuance signals strong institutional appetite for investment-grade renewable energy paper and may open refinancing exits for PE sponsors holding earlier-stage Iberian solar assets.

title: Copenhagen Airport Eyes €800m Terminal Expansion with New PE Partner
summary: Copenhagen Airports (CPH) is in exclusive negotiations with a major European infrastructure fund to finance an €800m international terminal expansion, with the new investor expected to take a 15–20% minority stake alongside OTPP and Macquarie. The deal reflects sustained PE interest in core European airport assets as passenger volumes recover to pre-pandemic highs.
